title: pseudibatia lanosa
intro: Pseudibatia lanosa, also known as the hairy pseudibatia, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Philippines. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pseudibatia lanosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe sore throats. The herbal preparation of the plant typically involves boiling the roots or leaves in water to create a tea or infusion, which is then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
